assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: The response clearly labels dev clusters as Rapid channel and prod as Stable throughout, with specific checkboxes distinguishing between them and mentions the timing advantage of Rapid channel upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions no node pool management, no SSH access to nodes, and emphasizes mandatory resource requests as critical for Autopilot

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Includes check for deprecated API usage via GKE deprecation insights dashboard and mentions reviewing breaking changes, though specific kubectl commands for API deprecation are not provided

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'All containers have resource requests (CPU/memory) - mandatory for Autopilot' and mentions it multiple times as a critical requirement

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Includes PDBs configuration, no bare pods requirement, and terminationGracePeriodSeconds limit in the workload readiness section

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud and kubectl commands to verify control plane version and node versions

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Comprehensive workload health section with specific kubectl commands to check deployments, pods, StatefulSets, PVCs, and application health

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions using dev clusters as validation and canary, leveraging the Rapid channel timing advantage

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Only mentions 72h advance notice for auto-upgrade notifications but does not discuss checking GKE release schedule for longer-term planning (~1 month advance notice)
Includes comprehensive observability section with metrics pipeline, logs, error rates, API latency, and Cloud Monitoring dashboard checks

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Only mentions maintenance exclusions for timing control ('No upgrades' for code freeze periods) and does not recommend node-level maintenance exclusions, correctly focusing on control plane upgrade timing
